meaning: adj.吹嘘的,傲慢的n.自吹自擂
examples: Another man was vaunting to a stout farmer the excellence of his shoes.
He raised his battle axe, and rising in his stirrups cut down the vaunting foe.
WITH Turkey vaunting itself as a model for the Arab world, the tendency is to see its 50-year-old goal of joining the European Union as dead.
Fights on television, the advent of a new generation of populist matadors, the vaunting of Spanish tradition as the country grows more confident: all have brought people back to the bulls.
